item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0), as it comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly the amount of -1s you got surprises me. This is a generally well written scp, that’s funny and interesting to read. Maybe it could be a -j but it’s still a +1 from me anyday

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was quite entertaining. I sometimes feel we need more funny SCP's. Not everything can be grimdark and horrible and evil. Sometimes we just need something stupid and funny.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the reply! The author of 3994, Dog Teeth, has stated:

I'll accept it, as it appears genuine. I appreciate their quick response.

All's well that ends well.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Got it. Again, I am more than happy to alter some things if the author of 3994 wishes me to.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
I'll rendezvous with the author of 3994 and see what they think. Given that we've got thousands of articles on the site, there's bound to be a bit of overlap, and I'm personally alright with this and 3994 coexisting as they are.

But yeah, gonna message 3994's author first.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
I had no idea that this skip (3994) had even existed prior to this admin post. I had simply thought of the idea because of my interest in the cliche extraterrestrial, being practical blobs of goo that try to be humans. I had simply combined that with the thought that they could be very incompetent, as we have not been invaded yet, and that could act as an explanation (as in why they have not succeeded yet).

As described in my author commentary on my author page, I was also listening to music and I wanted to incorporate that into my skip. Which combined with the incompetent thought and the music thought to form what if they just like our music, and that's why they haven't fully invaded as they want us to keep making more?

Coming from the author, this is entirely a coincidence, but I would be more than happy to change the concept if that is what's needed.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Author, a concern has been raised regarding this article, specifically that it seems to possess a couple similarities to a work that was posted in 2017, SCP-3994.

Of note:

Aside from concept (formation of shells/skin), there are some containment procedure similarities (ex. "15 per chamber" vs "12 per chamber") and other description similarities (having an amorphous and gelatinous/viscous mass disguised as a human, poor grammar/hold of human language, inability to pass as a human being, formation and decay of a shell, interview between an instance and a researcher).

This seems to be due to coincidence, so I'm not particularly inclined to order you to change it; however, since the concern was raised I believe it would be good to hear an explanation from you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At a certain point I began visualizing the SCP as one of the Amoeba Boys from "Powerpuff Girls" and now I can't stop.

Definitely a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aside from the awkward bits here and there regarding tone, making the piece fail for me as a professional-grade document, there's not even any creativity in showcasing how bad they are at emulating rapping.

It's all 'tell', so there's not much to appreciate, just lame vulgarity with no substance to back it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You ain't really wild, you a tourist

+1 lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will be refreshing the article with some tone polishing later tonight.

e: I have already polished it up a bit. Be sure to keep checking today for more and more tonal polishes!

item.title: Haha
item.content:encoded: +1. Corroborating with the others here that there are some minor tonal issues here. Not enough for me to dislike it but enough for me to notice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not just you, the tone's definitely off in this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it in general, however my main issue is mainly due to inexperience (not that I expect you to be an expert or anything). More or less, this article doesn't have a particularly "clinical" tone; using words like "extremely", "incredibly", and randomly using the word "extraterrestrial" when at no time in the article was it established that these entities are alien in nature. Also, it's not shown how they know the number of instances in existence, unless that's a reference to something that I missed. The interview was pretty much fine, I'd leave that the way it is.

But hey, for a first article, this wasn't half bad. I'll throw you an upvote anyway, good job

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a good idea, but I'm concerned about the writing in a few places:

"Large group… ~450" 450 isn't an especially large population for any known organism, so what metric are we using here?

"Gelatinous substance… does not confine to any defined shape" conformed is a better term than confined here.

"Obviously extraterrestrial". What about these creatures tells me, at a glance, they obviously aren't some terrestrial species that we simply haven't encountered yet? Or that they aren't simply mutated amoeba?

"Large degree of utter failure" is not clinical tone. Also it's redundant, as utter is synonymous with complete, negating any chance of success.

Also, I don't see hostility here. Rudeness? Certainly. But not hostility, as such.

It's little things like that that take away a bit from the article. I still love the idea and it's effects. I just need to see more polish before I can upvote

item.title: Re: Author Post: Incompetant Rap-Loving Bodysnatchers
item.content:encoded:
Some things you noted have been edited.

I appreciate your feedback!

item.title: Re: Author Post: Incompetant Rap-Loving Bodysnatchers
item.content:encoded:
This is a good, short SCP with an original idea, but some things are nagging me. Calling the SCP "possibly extraterrestrial" seems unnecessary to me. The foundation doesn't normally go around calling SCPs extraterrestrial, and there is seemingly no reason in the article as to why this SCP is specifically called that. I suggest either changing "extraterrestrial" to "anomalous" or adding some reason as to why the SCP would be called that.

The concept of the SCP living in hives is also bothering me. I would think the place the SCP lives would be more important to its containment than just one sentence mentioning it. You only name the word "hive" and don't elaborate what you mean by this at all, besides just a footnote. You say that the SCP lives in abandoned motels, but don't say anything else about it. What are these homes like? How do these homes come to be? Have foundation agents investigated one of these homes? Also, why are they called "hives" specifically? That's the foundation's designation for the home of these SCPs, but I don't see any reason for why they would call them "hives". Are the homes similar to hives made by wasps or bees or something of the like?

The last thing that irks me about the article is the last part of the paragraph detailing the SCP's ability to mimic someone. The sentence "While instances are disguised, they walk abnormally as compared to other bipedal organisms and maintain an odd posture" doesn't sound like something a professional foundation scientist would write. I you should alter it to something like "However, while instances are disguised, their gait is easily distinguishable from the typical human due to their abnormal posture." The last sentence feels like it could easily be removed, as the previous sentence makes it obvious that it would be easy to tell an SCP-3527 entity apart from a normal human.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Awehh, you're too kind. :3c

I'm happy to give the upvote! Best of luck in your future endeavors, and welcome to the site!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! It makes me very happy to see one of my absolute favorite authors upvoted my first attempt at an article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I'm a huge fan of short and simple SCPs, and unusual interview dialogue. This had my curiosity at the start of the description, my interest at the end of the description, and my upvote at "Dr. Prospero: I'm sorry to break it to you, but you are not Tupac."

Cheers for your first article being a success! +1 from me.

item.title: Author Post: Incompetent Rap-Loving Bodysnatchers
item.content:encoded:
I hope you all enjoyed my piece, as it is my first article on the site.

Thank you for reading and honest critique.
